affected_groups: children in underserved rural and northeastern counties
content.decision_question: Is regionally targeted workforce expansion sufficient on its own to close access gaps, or must it be paired with gatekeeping and finance reform to ensure added capacity translates into actual service delivery?
content.evidence_boundary: Future data on training completion, retention, and delivered habilitation hours could clarify whether workforce investment alone converts to service access. It cannot settle whether Hungary should also pursue simultaneous structural reform, since that involves competing judgments about reform sequencing and risk tolerance, not just capacity measurement.
content.tension: Targeted specialist workforce expansion (TP-live-t5) directly addresses documented regional shortages and is broadly politically uncontroversial, but rising training enrollment does not reliably convert into practicing specialists, and general salary reforms may not reach specialist supply, raising the question of whether targeted capacity investment alone can close access gaps without deeper organizational or entitlement change.
content.title: Workforce expansion targeting versus broad reform sufficiency
value_poles: structural reform of entitlement/access rules as a necessary complement
